---
type: fact
situation: When building a command-line application in Go
subject: Architecture
predicate: separates pure core from a thin shell across five files
object: 'Split into: (1) types.go — domain structs + sentinel errors only; (2) store.go — Store interface + FileStore + Clock interface + RealClock, no business logic; (3) core.go — App struct with all business logic, no os.* or time.Now() calls; (4) display.go — printTable(w io.Writer, items []T, useColor bool, today time.Time) with today injected for determinism; (5) main.go — DataDir(), dispatch map, command handlers, wiring. Command handlers in main.go call App methods then display functions — they are the only layer that touches flag.FlagSet and os.Exit.'
luhmann: "2"
created: "2026-05-30"
source: distilled L2 from build episodes
---

Information learned: when in building a command-line application in Go, Architecture separates pure core from a thin shell across five files Split into: (1) types.go — domain structs + sentinel errors only; (2) store.go — Store interface + FileStore + Clock interface + RealClock, no business logic; (3) core.go — App struct with all business logic, no os.* or time.Now() calls; (4) display.go — printTable(w io.Writer, items []T, useColor bool, today time.Time) with today injected for determinism; (5) main.go — DataDir(), dispatch map, command handlers, wiring. Command handlers in main.go call App methods then display functions — they are the only layer that touches flag.FlagSet and os.Exit..



**Accumulated (bookmarks):** Accumulated from bookmarks build: the five-file split is not strict — a four-file variant is valid (bookmark.go = types+sentinel errors, store.go, core.go with the display helper inlined, main.go) when display logic is minimal and needs no injected today. A separate display.go is optional.
